content.text:
Here are some of the biggest **AI-related headlines circulating today, Thursday, April 9, 2026**, based on recent coverage I could verify. I’m treating “today” as **April 9, 2026** specifically.

1. **OpenAI Foundation shares new leadership and rollout plans**
   - OpenAI said on **March 24, 2026** that co-founder **Wojciech Zaremba** is joining the Foundation as Head of AI Resilience, and **Anna Makanju** is set to join in mid-April as Head of AI for Civil Society and Philanthropy. That’s one of the more recent official OpenAI announcements still getting attention. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/update-on-the-openai-foundation//?utm_source=openai))

2. **OpenAI expands government/national-security work with GenAI.mil**
   - In a **February 9, 2026** announcement, OpenAI said it is bringing ChatGPT to **GenAI.mil**, describing it as the Department of War’s secure enterprise AI platform used by **3 million civilian and military personnel**. That remains a significant AI policy and deployment story. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/bringing-chatgpt-to-genaimil/?utm_source=openai))

3. **OpenAI and SoftBank-linked SB Energy push AI + energy infrastructure**
   - On **January 9, 2026**, OpenAI announced a strategic partnership with **SB Energy** as part of **Stargate**, framing it as a step in building next-generation AI and energy infrastructure in the United States. AI power demand and infrastructure continue to be a top headline theme. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/stargate-sb-energy-partnership//?utm_source=openai))

4. **TechCrunch spotlight: AI’s impact on jobs and labor in 2026**
   - TechCrunch reported that investors increasingly expect AI to affect labor markets in 2026, including pressure on hiring and more automation of repetitive and some higher-logic work. That’s more analysis than breaking news, but it is one of the clearest “what matters now” AI headlines. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2025/12/31/investors-predict-ai-is-coming-for-labor-in-2026/?utm_source=openai))

5. **TechCrunch theme: AI shifts from hype to pragmatism**
   - Another current TechCrunch framing is that **2026 will be about practical deployment**, including physical AI, robotics, autonomous systems, and enterprise implementation rather than pure model hype. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/02/in-2026-ai-will-move-from-hype-to-pragmatism/?utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can do a **more rigorous “top 10 AI headlines today” roundup from major news outlets only** — for example just **Reuters, AP, Bloomberg, TechCrunch, and The Verge** — with a cleaner ranked list and one-line summaries.
